# Insider One Case Study — Marketing Journey Builder

> This file follows the llms.txt standard (https://llmstxt.org) to provide AI assistants with structured, reliable context about this project.

## Project

A production-grade **Marketing Automation Journey Builder** built as a case study for Insider One's frontend developer position. The application simulates Insider One's core product: a visual, cross-channel customer journey orchestration platform.

## Purpose

Allow marketing teams to design automated customer journeys using a node-based canvas:
- **Triggers**: Events that start a journey (user signs up, adds to cart, visits page)
- **Conditions**: Branching logic (has mobile app?, cart value > 1000 TL?)  
- **Actions**: Cross-channel communications (WhatsApp, SMS, Email, Push)
- **Wait nodes**: Time delays between steps
- **Split nodes**: A/B testing branches

## Tech Stack

- Vue 3 + TypeScript (Strict) + Vite + pnpm
- Pinia (state management, Setup Store syntax only)
- Vue Router (lazy-loaded routes)
- Tailwind CSS (utility-first, no inline styles)
- MSW (Mock Service Worker for API simulation)
- VueUse (composables library)
- Vitest + @vue/test-utils (unit + component tests)
- Playwright (E2E tests)
- Storybook (component documentation)
- Sentry (error monitoring)
- GitHub Actions + AWS Amplify (CI/CD)

## Architecture

Feature-driven modular architecture. Each feature owns its components, store, types, composables, and tests. Shared UI lives in `src/components/base/` with `Ins` prefix naming.

## Key Files

- `CLAUDE.md` — Full developer guide and architecture decisions
- `.cursorrules` — AI assistant rules (Cursor)
- `.cursor/rules/` — Granular Cursor rule files per concern
- `.skills/` — Task-specific AI skill guides
- `src/types/journey.ts` — Core domain type definitions
- `src/core/mocks/handlers.ts` — MSW API mock handlers
- `src/features/journey/` — Main feature module

## Coding Standards

- Zero `any` in TypeScript
- `<script setup>` syntax only (no Options API)
- Props typed with `defineProps<Interface>()` generic syntax
- Stores use Setup Store syntax (`defineStore('id', () => { ... })`)
- All async actions have `isLoading`, `error`, `try/catch/finally`
- Every component has a co-located `.test.ts` file
- Every base component has a `.stories.ts` Storybook file

## Optional

- `docs/` — Architecture decisions and AI collaboration log
- `.env.example` — Required environment variables
